user_query:
I’m planning school visits and revisions for my tech-themed children’s stories, and I want to automate my follow-up reminders. Please use the provided inputs to generate one CSV of email reminders and to update my weekly reminders document.

Inputs to read:
- input/school_visits.csv (visit schedule)
- input/manuscripts.json (work-in-progress stories)
- input/email_template.md (email template with placeholders)
- docs/REMINDERS.md (existing reminders doc to update)

Deliverables:
1) Create out/visit_reminders.csv with one row per confirmed school visit. Include only rows where status is "confirmed". Columns (in this order):
   - id
   - school
   - contact_name
   - contact_email
   - visit_date (from input, YYYY-MM-DD)
   - start_time (from input)
   - timezone (from input)
   - grade_band (from input)
   - theme_focus (from input)
   - reminder_send_date (visit_date minus 3 calendar days, formatted YYYY-MM-DD)
   - subject (rendered from input/email_template.md)
   - body (full rendered message from input/email_template.md)

   Fill the template placeholders as follows:
   - {{SCHOOL}} → school
   - {{CONTACT_NAME}} → contact_name
   - {{DATE}} → visit_date
   - {{TIME}} → start_time
   - {{TIMEZONE}} → timezone
   - {{THEME}} → theme_focus
   - {{GRADE_BAND}} → grade_band

   Sort rows by reminder_send_date ascending. The number of rows must equal the number of confirmed visits in input/school_visits.csv.

2) Update docs/REMINDERS.md by rewriting exactly the two sections titled "## Upcoming School Visits" and "## Manuscript Edits" and leaving all other content unchanged.
   - In "## Upcoming School Visits", replace existing bullets with one bullet per confirmed visit, sorted by reminder_send_date ascending. Each bullet should include: [reminder_send_date], contact_name, contact_email, school, visit_date, start_time, timezone, theme_focus, and grade_band. Example pattern (adapt text as needed without omitting fields):
     - [YYYY-MM-DD] Remind CONTACT_NAME (CONTACT_EMAIL) about SCHOOL visit on VISIT_DATE START_TIME TIMEZONE (theme: THEME_FOCUS, grades GRADE_BAND).
     The count of bullets here must match the number of rows in out/visit_reminders.csv.
   - In "## Manuscript Edits", replace existing bullets with one bullet per manuscript whose status is not "completed" and whose next_edit_deadline is present (non-null), sorted by next_edit_deadline ascending. Each bullet should include: [next_edit_deadline], title, theme, status, and a short note derived from notes. Example pattern (adapt text as needed without omitting fields):
     - [YYYY-MM-DD] TITLE (THEME) — status: STATUS. Notes: NOTES.

Assumptions/constraints:
- Compute reminder_send_date strictly as (visit_date minus 3 days); no time-of-day math is needed.
- Do not add or remove any other sections in docs/REMINDERS.md.
- Keep the email body multi-line as rendered from the template; include the full body text in the body column.
- Ensure consistent sorting and that the visits section and the CSV reflect the same set of confirmed visits.

Output paths to create/modify:
- out/visit_reminders.csv (new file)
- docs/REMINDERS.md (edited in place)

Let me know if any template placeholder is missing a corresponding field in the inputs, but proceed with the rest. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
